name: api-designer
description: An extension which gives a development environment for designing, developing, debugging, and testing API solutions.

languageModelTools.name: api-designer_validateAPISpec
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validates an API specification (OpenAPI or AsyncAPI) against standard rules using Spectral. Automatically detects spec type and applies appropriate ruleset.

apiSpec.description: The API specification to validate (can be YAML/JSON string or object). Works with both OpenAPI and AsyncAPI.

fileUri.description: Optional: URI of the file containing the API specification. If provided, this takes precedence over apiSpec.

languageModelTools.name: api-designer_validateWithSpectralRuleset
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validates an API specification (OpenAPI or AsyncAPI) against a custom dynamic ruleset (e.g., governance rules, AI readiness rules)

apiSpec.description: The API specification to validate (YAML/JSON string). Works with both OpenAPI and AsyncAPI.

fileUri.description: Optional: URI of the file containing the API specification. If provided, this takes precedence over apiSpec.

rulesetName.description: Name of the ruleset to use for validation

fileUrl.description: URL or path to the ruleset file

rulesetContentPath.description: Path to the ruleset content within the file

gitRootPath.description: Optional: Root path of the git repository if ruleset is in a git repo

authToken.description: Optional: Authentication token for accessing the ruleset

languageModelTools.name: api-designer_convertOpenAPIToWSO2YAML
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Converts an OpenAPI specification to WSO2 API Platform YAML format

apiSpec.description: The OpenAPI specification to convert (can be YAML/JSON string or object)

fileUri.description: Optional: URI of the file containing the OpenAPI specification. If provided, this takes precedence over apiSpec.

existingArtifact.description: Optional: Existing WSO2 artifact to merge with (preserves custom policies and configurations)

userProvidedName.description: Optional: Custom API name (overrides OpenAPI info.title)

userProvidedVersion.description: Optional: Custom API version (overrides OpenAPI info.version)

userProvidedDescription.description: Optional: Custom API description (overrides OpenAPI info.description)

viewsWelcome.contents:
Welcome to API Designer. To start working, please open a folder.
[Open Folder](command:vscode.openFolder)

No OpenAPI files found in this workspace.
[Create New API](command:api-designer.createOpenAPIFromPanel)

apiDesigner.debugLog.description: Enable printing debug messages to the API Designer output channel

apiDesigner.ai.provider.description: AI provider to use for code generation and assistance

apiDesigner.spectral.rulesetFolders.description: GitHub folder URLs or local paths containing Spectral rulesets. Adding a new folder here triggers the extension to list the contained rulesets for selection.
apiDesigner.spectral.rulesetFolders.markdownDescription:
GitHub folder URLs or local paths containing Spectral rulesets. When you add a new entry, the extension automatically scans the folder and lets you choose which rulesets to enable.

The default entry must match `DEFAULT_SPECTRAL_RULESET_CATALOG_FOLDER_URL` in `@wso2/api-designer-core` (change both when switching the canonical ruleset catalog).

apiDesigner.spectral.selectedRulesets.description: Rulesets discovered from the configured folders and whether they are enabled.

sourceFolder.description: The source folder URL or path the ruleset was discovered from

rulesetContentPath.description: Optional JSONPath inside the YAML where the ruleset content resides

enabled.description: Whether the ruleset should be used when initializing an API project

scripts.build: pnpm clean && pnpm run copyFonts && webpack --mode production --devtool hidden-source-map && pnpm run package && pnpm run postbuild
scripts.pretest: pnpm run compile-tests && pnpm run compile && pnpm run lint
scripts.e2e-test-setup: npx extest get-vscode -c 1.81.1 && npx extest get-chromedriver -c 1.81.1 && npx extest install-vsix -f $(ls vsix/*.vsix)
scripts.e2e-test: pnpm run compile-tests && pnpm exec playwright test
